cbas.title: Collective Bargaining Agreement 2025–2027
cbas.description: Most recent collective bargaining agreement covering wages, scheduling, discipline, health benefits, and working conditions for sworn and civilian members from 2025 through 2027.

cbas.title: Collective Bargaining Agreement 2022–2024
cbas.description: Prior agreement in effect from 2022 through 2024, superseded by the 2025–2027 CBA.

cbas.title: Collective Bargaining Agreement 2019–2021
cbas.description: Historic collective bargaining agreement that introduced major changes to overtime and scheduling provisions.

cbas.title: Collective Bargaining Agreement 2016–2018
cbas.description: Legacy agreement maintained for archival reference and comparison to more recent CBAs.

districts.name: District 1 – Downtown
districts.description: Covers the downtown core, central business district, and adjoining transit hubs.

districts.name: District 2 – North Precincts
districts.description: Represents members assigned to northern precincts, traffic units, and airport detail.

districts.name: District 3 – Central & Mid-City
districts.description: Central and mid-city patrol areas, including major commercial corridors and special operations units.

districts.name: District 4 – South & Residential
districts.description: Southside residential neighborhoods, school resource officers, and community policing units.

districts.name: District 5 – Outlying & Specialized
districts.description: Outlying jurisdictions, joint task forces, and specialized investigative units.

health_plans.name: Blue Shield Individual Value 500
health_plans.summary: Mid-premium individual PPO plan with a low $500 annual deductible and broad provider network.

health_plans.name: HealthFirst Individual Standard 1000
health_plans.summary: Affordable individual HMO option with a $1,000 deductible and coordinated care network.

health_plans.name: SecureCare Individual Budget 2000
health_plans.summary: Lowest-cost individual plan with higher deductible suited for members who use care infrequently.

health_plans.name: MetroPlus Individual Premium 250
health_plans.summary: High-end individual PPO plan with very low deductible and enhanced out-of-network coverage.

health_plans.name: UnionChoice Individual HSA 3000
health_plans.summary: HSA-eligible high deductible individual plan with coinsurance after the deductible instead of copays.

health_plans.name: Guardian Individual Basic 1500
health_plans.summary: Balanced individual plan with moderate premiums and deductible, including nationwide coverage.

health_plans.name: Blue Shield Family Standard 1500
health_plans.summary: Family PPO coverage with a shared deductible and strong in-network benefits.

health_plans.name: HealthFirst Family Value 2500
health_plans.summary: Budget-friendly family HMO plan with higher deductible and lower monthly premium.

health_plans.name: MetroPlus Employee & Spouse Premium 500
health_plans.summary: Rich coverage for employee and spouse with low deductible and competitive copays.

health_plans.name: UnionChoice Employee & Spouse HSA 3000
health_plans.summary: HSA-eligible plan for employee and spouse that emphasizes lower premiums and savings contributions.

health_plans.name: Guardian Employee & Children Standard 1000
health_plans.summary: Popular choice for members covering children with a moderate deductible and competitive premiums.

health_plans.name: SecureCare Employee & Children Value 2000
health_plans.summary: Lower-premium HMO designed for members primarily using in-network pediatric and urgent care.

health_plans.name: Retiree Advantage 750
health_plans.summary: Plan tailored for retired members with Medicare coordination and enhanced prescription coverage.

health_plans.name: Legacy Individual 1500
health_plans.summary: Older individual plan kept for grandfathered members; closed to new enrollment.

health_plans.name: UnionChoice Individual Core 1250
health_plans.summary: Core individual plan designed for union members, balancing cost and comprehensive coverage.

interest_options.name: Training
interest_options.description: In-person and online trainings on officer safety, de-escalation, leadership, and more.

interest_options.name: Legal Updates
interest_options.description: Updates on case law, collective bargaining changes, and legal guidance for members.

interest_options.name: Wellness
interest_options.description: Resources and programs focused on mental health, physical fitness, and resiliency.

interest_options.name: Union News
interest_options.description: General union announcements, contract news, and leadership messages.

interest_options.name: Benefits & Discounts
interest_options.description: Information about member benefits, insurance, and vendor discounts.

interest_options.name: Community Outreach
interest_options.description: Opportunities to volunteer in community events and charitable programs.

membership_categories.name: Sworn Officer – Full-time
membership_categories.description: Full-time sworn law enforcement officers currently employed by a participating agency.

membership_categories.name: Sworn Officer – Part-time/Reserve
membership_categories.description: Part-time, reserve, or auxiliary sworn officers with reduced dues options.

membership_categories.name: Civilian Professional Staff
membership_categories.description: Non-sworn civilian employees working in law enforcement agencies.

membership_categories.name: Retired Member
membership_categories.description: Retired sworn or civilian members who wish to maintain union affiliation.

membership_categories.name: Associate Member
membership_categories.description: Non-bargaining-unit individuals approved for limited associate membership.

cba_articles.title: Recognition and Bargaining Unit
cba_articles.content: Article 1 establishes the Union as the exclusive bargaining representative for all sworn officers and designated civilian classifications employed by participating agencies, excluding those positions defined as management, confidential, or otherwise exempt under state law. The bargaining unit definition in this Article governs eligibility for union membership, representation rights, and coverage under the collective bargaining agreement.

cba_articles.title: Union Security and Dues Deduction
cba_articles.content: Article 2 sets forth provisions regarding union membership, dues authorization, and revocation procedures consistent with applicable law. The Employer agrees to deduct union dues, initiation fees, and authorized assessments from the paychecks of members who submit valid authorizations. Deductions shall be remitted to the Union on a biweekly basis with accompanying reports. This Article also describes procedures for changes in dues amounts and for members who transfer, separate, or retire.

cba_articles.title: Hours of Work and Overtime
cba_articles.content: Article 5 defines the normal workweek, shift lengths, and overtime eligibility for both sworn and civilian classifications. It includes provisions on shift bidding, mandatory overtime, call-back pay, court time, and compensatory time. The Article requires that non-emergency schedule changes for regular shifts be provided with a minimum of 72 hours' notice, except where otherwise negotiated for specialized units. Overtime assignments must be distributed as equitably as practicable and tracked on an overtime roster available for member review.

cba_articles.title: Health and Welfare Benefits
cba_articles.content: Article 10 outlines the health, dental, vision, and life insurance benefits provided to members and eligible dependents. The Employer agrees to offer a menu of health plans, including HMO, PPO, and HSA-compatible options, with defined premium-sharing percentages. Changes to carriers or benefit levels during the term of the Agreement require bargaining with the Union. This Article also specifies open enrollment timelines, eligibility for new hires, and continuation of coverage for retired members.

cba_articles.title: Grievance and Arbitration Procedure
cba_articles.content: Article 12 establishes a multi-step grievance procedure for resolving disputes involving the interpretation or application of the Agreement. It sets time limits for filing and appealing grievances, identifies the levels of supervision at each step, and permits representation by the Union at all stages. Unresolved grievances may be submitted to binding arbitration, and the Article details arbitrator selection, hearing procedures, and the scope of the arbitrator's authority.

cba_articles.title: Discipline
cba_articles.content:
Article 15 governs the standards and procedures for discipline imposed on bargaining unit members. Discipline shall be for just cause and applied in a manner consistent with the principles of progressive discipline, taking into account the nature of the alleged misconduct, prior record, and mitigating circumstances. The Article requires that members be provided with written notice of proposed discipline, the factual basis for the action, and an opportunity to respond before final decisions are made, except in cases of emergency suspension.

The Article sets out investigative timelines, access to investigative files, and the right of a member to have Union representation present during investigatory interviews that may lead to discipline. It clarifies that discipline may range from counseling and written reprimands to suspension, demotion, or termination. Records of discipline are maintained in the member's personnel file subject to negotiated retention periods.

This Article also cross-references the Grievance and Arbitration Procedure, confirming that disciplinary actions may be grieved and, if not resolved, submitted to arbitration. Nothing in this Article limits the rights of members to seek relief under applicable law, provided that contractual timelines are observed.

cba_articles.title: Safety and Equipment
cba_articles.content: Article 20 addresses the Employer's obligation to provide safe working conditions, personal protective equipment, and properly maintained vehicles and communications systems. It includes standards for patrol car equipment, ballistic vests, body-worn cameras, and other essential safety gear. The Employer must consult with the Union prior to making substantial changes to issued equipment. Members may raise safety concerns through the Labor-Management Committee or the grievance procedure where appropriate.

cba_articles.title: Recognition and Bargaining Unit
cba_articles.content: Article 1 of the 20222024 Agreement designates the Union as the exclusive bargaining agent for the defined bargaining unit and lists classifications included and excluded from coverage. It also addresses recognition of newly created classifications during the term of the Agreement.

cba_articles.title: Discipline
cba_articles.content: Article 15 of the 20222024 Agreement sets out the just cause standard for discipline and general procedures for notice, response, and appeal. While similar to the 20252027 language, timelines and record-retention provisions differ. This Article is retained for reference in cases involving conduct occurring under the prior Agreement.

cba_articles.title: Hours of Work and Overtime
cba_articles.content: Article 5 of the 20192021 Agreement, now superseded, provided earlier rules on shift schedules, overtime, and call-back pay. Some provisions were substantially revised in later Agreements in response to member feedback and changes in operational needs.

cba_articles.title: Health and Welfare Benefits
cba_articles.content: This Article describes the health and welfare benefits available during the 20162018 Agreement period, including prior plan options and employer contribution levels.

dues_plans.name: Full-time Sworn 1 Value Monthly
dues_plans.description: Discounted monthly dues option for full-time sworn officers, providing full representation and benefits at the lowest monthly rate.

dues_plans.name: Full-time Sworn 1 Standard Monthly
dues_plans.description: Standard monthly dues plan for full-time sworn officers, including full access to union services and legal representation.

dues_plans.name: Full-time Sworn 1 Premium Monthly
dues_plans.description: Premium monthly dues plan that supports additional political advocacy and hardship funds.

dues_plans.name: Full-time Sworn 1 Quarterly
dues_plans.description: Quarterly dues billing option for full-time sworn officers, equivalent to the standard monthly rate.

dues_plans.name: Full-time Sworn 1 Annual
dues_plans.description: Annual dues payment option at a slight discount compared to monthly billing for full-time sworn officers.

dues_plans.name: Part-time / Reserve Sworn 1 Monthly
dues_plans.description: Reduced monthly dues for part-time and reserve sworn officers.

dues_plans.name: Part-time / Reserve Sworn 1 Annual
dues_plans.description: Annual payment option for part-time and reserve sworn officers.

dues_plans.name: Civilian Staff 1 Standard Monthly
dues_plans.description: Standard monthly dues plan for civilian professional staff.

dues_plans.name: Civilian Staff 1 Value Monthly
dues_plans.description: Lower-cost monthly dues option for civilian staff with the same representation coverage.

dues_plans.name: Civilian Staff 1 Annual
dues_plans.description: Annual dues option for civilian staff members.

dues_plans.name: Retired Member 1 Monthly
dues_plans.description: Monthly dues for retired members who wish to stay connected and retain certain benefits.

dues_plans.name: Retired Member 1 Annual
dues_plans.description: Annual dues for retired members at a discounted rate compared to monthly billing.

dues_plans.name: Associate Member 1 Annual
dues_plans.description: Annual dues plan for associate members. New enrollments may be limited based on union policy.

dues_plans.name: Full-time Sworn 1 Initiation Fee
dues_plans.description: One-time initiation fee applicable to new full-time sworn officer members.

representatives.name: Carla Martinez
representatives.title: District 1 Representative
representatives.bio: Carla is a senior patrol officer assigned to the Downtown Precinct with over 12 years of service. She focuses on fair scheduling practices and officer wellness resources.

representatives.name: Marcus Lee
representatives.title: District 2 Representative
representatives.bio: Marcus represents members in the North Precincts and airport detail, with a focus on travel time compensation and specialized assignment pay.

representatives.name: Danielle Brooks
representatives.title: District 3 Representative
representatives.bio: Danielle serves as the primary representative for District 3, covering Central and Mid-City areas. Her priorities include protecting contractual overtime rights and ensuring fair access to equipment and training.

representatives.name: Ethan Johnson
representatives.title: District 4 Representative
representatives.bio: Ethan represents South & Residential District 4 members, with emphasis on school resource officer protections and community policing support.

representatives.name: Priya Desai
representatives.title: District 5 Representative
representatives.bio: Priya advocates for members in outlying and specialized units, focusing on travel, on-call, and hazardous duty issues.

representatives.name: Alex Chen
representatives.title: Union President
representatives.bio: Alex is the elected Union President, responsible for overall leadership, contract negotiations, and representing the membership with city leadership.

representatives.name: Renee Alvarez
representatives.title: Executive Vice President
representatives.bio: Renee oversees member services, including benefits, training, and internal grievance support.

representatives.name: Thomas Green
representatives.title: Labor Relations Staff Liaison
representatives.bio: Thomas is a full-time union staff member who supports district representatives with research, data, and contract enforcement.

events.title: Officer Safety Foundations (Denver)
events.description: Full-day in-person course covering patrol officer safety fundamentals, threat assessment, contact and cover principles, and recent case studies relevant to urban policing.

events.title: Advanced Officer Safety & Tactics (Denver)
events.description: Advanced in-person training focused on high-risk stops, low-light encounters, and integrating body-worn camera review into safety debriefs.

events.title: De-escalation and Communication Skills Workshop
events.description: Half-day interactive workshop emphasizing communication, crisis intervention, and de-escalation strategies aligned with current best practices.

events.title: Officer Safety Update: Recent Case Law (Online)
events.description: Live webinar reviewing recent officer safety-related case law, policy updates, and lessons learned from critical incidents.

events.title: Officer Wellness & Resilience Training
events.description: In-person training on stress management, peer support, and wellness resources available through the union.

events.title: Advanced Field Tactics for Patrol Supervisors
events.description: Scenario-based training for sergeants and acting supervisors on supervising high-risk incidents and coordinating resources.

events.title: General Membership Union Meeting – March 2026
events.description: Monthly union meeting to review bargaining updates, grievances, training opportunities, and committee reports.

events.title: General Membership Union Meeting – April 2026
events.description: Regular weekday evening meeting with updates on contract enforcement, PAC activity, and upcoming trainings.

events.title: Special Meeting – Contract Ratification Vote
events.description: Special union meeting to present the tentative agreement and conduct a ratification vote of the latest CBA.

events.title: Union Steward Training & Orientation
events.description: Full-day orientation for new and prospective union stewards on contract enforcement, grievances, and member outreach.

events.title: PAC Town Hall and Endorsement Discussion
events.description: Open forum with union leadership to discuss PAC priorities, candidate endorsements, and member questions.

events.title: Benefits Open Enrollment Overview
events.description: Online overview of health, dental, and vision plan options for the upcoming plan year, including premium and deductible comparisons.
